behavior_summary: When the player examines the hollow log, the object's U8P_EWHOLLOG::look handler displays a short descriptive line ('hollow log ') and then suspends the process.
No decisions, flags, parameters, or external calls are used — the behavior is a simple, single-line descriptive response tied to the 'U8P_EWHOLLOG::look' verb.
notable_control_flow: U8P_EWHOLLOG::look executes a single bark (player-facing text) and then immediately issues a suspend, ending/pausing the U8P_EWHOLLOG::look process.
There are no conditional branches, menu interactions, or subsequent actions in the routine.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'hollow log ' intentional (formatting/concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?

Could this U8P_EWHOLLOG::look handler also be triggered by fast-area entry semantics in addition to explicit player 'U8P_EWHOLLOG::look' actions? (engine may call U8P_EWHOLLOG::look-type processes on fast-area entry; inferred but unconfirmed)
Is there any higher-level context that modifies this description (different states, global flags, or localization) that the disassembly fragment does not show?
